purpose: Machine-readable contract for letting a code agent orchestrate Formica-Flow from literature input to multimodal dataset output.

readiness_summary.main_blocker: The remaining high-risk work is production validation of model-backed batch auto-annotation and final audit report generation.
readiness_summary.recommended_next_step: Run the contract on a small real PDF folder, then enable model inference with explicit approval and inspect the final dataset/provenance audit.

required_inputs.description: Folder containing source PDFs for literature screening and figure extraction.
Project JSON used by governance and dataset export stages.
SQLite database produced by PDF extraction.
Governance policy for routing, sampling, redline gates, and acceptance checks.
Output artifact root for agent-driven runs.

stages.title: Preflight and run plan
gates: All configured files exist or are explicitly marked as deferred.
The agent records a run id, input paths, assumptions, and blocked stages before touching data.
stages.agent_notes: This stage is implemented by tools/agentic/run_agentic_pipeline.py in dry-run mode.

stages.title: PDF screening
gates: Resume state is valid for the current PDF manifest.
LLM responses are saved as raw evidence.
Uncertain or unreadable PDFs are preserved as review artifacts rather than silently dropped.
stages.agent_notes: A headless CLI wrapper now exists. Runs still require a configured PDF source folder and real LLM settings for production use.

stages.title: Triptych figure extraction
gates: Real multimodal validator is configured or the run is explicitly marked as mock/default review.
Each accepted figure has page, figure index, image path, caption evidence, and review status.
Figures lacking reliable triptych evidence remain candidates, not trusted training samples.
stages.agent_notes: A headless CLI wrapper now exists. Real production runs should pass a multimodal config or explicitly disable multimodal validation.

stages.title: Candidate bridge, sampling, and routing
gates: Acceptance suite passes.
Candidate IDs are deduplicated.
Ambiguous routing is not promoted to Core-2 automatically.
stages.agent_notes: This is already the strongest automation island in the repo.

stages.title: Import routed figures into project
gates: Every imported image keeps source PDF, page, figure index, candidate id, and review status provenance.
Duplicate source figures are skipped idempotently.
Project taxonomy and locator scope are not silently expanded.
stages.agent_notes: A headless import adapter now exists and writes image_provenance into the output project.

stages.title: Batch locator and segmentation
gates: Prediction metadata records model weights and cascade route manifest.
Invalid boxes, empty masks, and out-of-bounds polygons are rejected before project writeback.
A deterministic report is emitted even when all models are assumed strong.
stages.agent_notes: A headless CLI now exists. Production use with --run-engine initializes model weights; tests can use --predictions to exercise writeback without GPU/model setup.

stages.title: Multimodal dataset export
gates: JSONL schema version and provenance fields are present.
All referenced image files exist.
Each sample has global image, local crop, text, label, segmentation, bbox, source lineage, and model provenance.
stages.agent_notes: A headless CLI wrapper now exists. The next improvement is adding source lineage and model provenance fields to each JSONL row.

stages.title: Final audit package
gates: All required artifacts are present.
Blocked/deferred stages are explicit.
The report separates trusted training data from candidates and diagnostics.
stages.agent_notes: This should be generated by the code agent after executing or dry-running the contract.

non_negotiable_controls: Do not let the agent operate the Qt GUI as the primary automation path.
All stages must be idempotent or write into run-scoped output directories.
Every accepted sample needs source provenance from PDF to project image to exported dataset row.
Mock/default multimodal review must never be silently treated as trusted acceptance.
A failed redline or acceptance gate blocks promotion, even if locator and annotation models are assumed excellent.
